title: PC Running Slow
steps: Restart your computer – this clears temporary files and refreshes memory.
Open Task Manager (Ctrl + Shift + Esc) and check for programs using high CPU or RAM.
Uninstall unused programs via Settings > Apps.
Run Disk Cleanup: search 'Disk Cleanup' in the Start menu and follow the wizard.
Check that your hard drive has at least 10% free space.
Scan for malware using Windows Defender (Settings > Windows Security > Virus & threat protection).
If the issue persists, consider upgrading RAM or switching to an SSD.

title: No Internet Connection
steps: Check that Wi-Fi or Ethernet is enabled on your device.
Restart your router and modem – unplug for 30 seconds then plug back in.
Forget the Wi-Fi network and reconnect: Settings > Network > Wi-Fi > Manage known networks.
Run the Windows Network Troubleshooter: Settings > System > Troubleshoot > Internet Connections.
Flush DNS cache: open Command Prompt as Administrator and run 'ipconfig /flushdns'.
Check if other devices can connect – if not, the issue is likely with the router/ISP.
Contact your Internet Service Provider if all steps fail.

title: Blue Screen of Death (BSOD)
steps: Note the error code shown on the blue screen (e.g., MEMORY_MANAGEMENT).
Restart your PC – a one-time BSOD is often caused by a minor glitch.
Check for recent Windows Updates: Settings > Windows Update.
Uninstall recently installed drivers or software that may have caused the crash.
Run Windows Memory Diagnostic: search 'Windows Memory Diagnostic' in Start.
Run 'sfc /scannow' in an elevated Command Prompt to repair system files.
If BSODs persist, back up your data and consider a Windows reset.

title: Overheating / Fan Noise
steps: Ensure your PC or laptop is on a hard, flat surface with good airflow.
Clean dust from the vents using compressed air.
Check CPU temperature with a free tool like HWMonitor or Core Temp.
Close background apps to reduce CPU load (Task Manager > CPU column).
Update drivers, especially GPU drivers, from the manufacturer's website.
Check that all fans are spinning (open the case or peek through laptop vents).
If a laptop, consider a cooling pad.
Seek professional help if thermal paste on the CPU needs replacement.

title: No Display / Black Screen
steps: Check that the monitor is powered on and the brightness is not set to zero.
Verify that the cable (HDMI, DisplayPort, or VGA) is firmly connected at both ends.
Try a different cable or a different monitor if available.
For desktops: if you have a dedicated GPU, ensure the cable is plugged into the GPU, not the motherboard.
Restart the PC and listen for beep codes that may indicate hardware issues.
Try booting in Safe Mode: hold Shift while clicking Restart > Troubleshoot > Advanced Options > Startup Settings.
If none of the above works, the GPU or monitor may need professional inspection.
